InformalRole: Coordinate type for one admissible local operation: Fano address, triadic role, and orientation.
CanonicalSource: KT admissible-operation construction; canonical source mapping pending
SourceStatement: A lawful local operation is indexed by one Fano address, one of three triadic roles, and one of two orientations.
LeanStatement: structure Operation where address : Fano.Point; role : TriadicRole; orientation : Orientation
DivergenceFromSource: Source citation and exact wording still require ledger completion.
PermittedAssumptions: Fano point structure; triadic-role type; orientation type
FailureCondition: A source audit showing that the three coordinates are not independent or do not exhaust the intended admissible operations would require revision.
AuditNotes: Cardinality is now proved without native_decide. The 42 count remains relative to this frozen coordinate definition.

InformalRole: Semantic framed operation whose orientation is recovered from the ordering of the two non-anchor Fano points.
CanonicalSource: KT framed-operation normal-form construction; canonical source mapping pending
SourceStatement: A lawful operation consists of a Fano address, an oriented assignment of triadic roles to the addressed line, and one active role.
LeanStatement: structure LawfulFramedOperation where address : Fano.Point; frame : TriadicRole → Fano.Point; activeRole : TriadicRole; frame_law : ...
DivergenceFromSource: Introduced in Lean to give the coordinate type an independent semantic realization. Exact conservativity is proved through the normal-form equivalence.
PermittedAssumptions: AdmissibleOperation definitions; Fano completion and successor structure
FailureCondition: Failure of the equivalence with AdmissibleOperation.Operation would break the claim that the 42 coordinate triples classify the framed law.
AuditNotes: Classification is relative to the explicit framed Fano law, not every conceivable KT-local process.

InformalRole: Canonical name for the previously proved lawful framed-operation space.
CanonicalSource: KT glyph construction; canonical source mapping pending
SourceStatement: A glyph is one lawful framed local operation with uniquely recoverable Fano address, role, and orientation.
DivergenceFromSource: No new carrier is introduced; Glyph is an abbreviation for the lawful framed-operation type.
FailureCondition: If the framed-operation law is not intrinsic or the normal-form equivalence fails, the glyph interpretation must be revised.
AuditNotes: The 42 count is a theorem about this exact carrier. Physical invariance under the concrete PSL(2,7) action remains unproved.

InformalRole: Complete pre-tokenization monad: one glyph together with one realized fourfold walk state.
CanonicalSource: KT monad construction; canonical source mapping pending
SourceStatement: A monad is a glyph together with one of four walk realizations determined by temporal direction and information phase.
DivergenceFromSource: The current Lean representation packages the walk realization as temporal direction × information phase. The exact source correspondence must be documented.
FailureCondition: If the four walk realizations are not exhaustive or do not transform coherently under PSL(2,7), the monad construction requires revision.
AuditNotes: The 168 count is now free of native_decide dependencies. Concrete PSL(2,7) equivariance remains open.

InformalRole: Target Cayley–Dickson multiplication on a pair of quaternion blocks.
CanonicalSource: Classical Cayley–Dickson construction; exact bibliographic source pending
SourceStatement: For x=(a,b) and y=(c,d), define x⋆y=(ac-conj(d)b, da+bconj(c)).
LeanStatement: def cdMul (x y : Carrier) := (x.1 * y.1 - star y.2 * x.2, y.2 * x.1 + x.2 * star y.1)
DivergenceFromSource: Exact sign and convention mapping to the chosen literature source must be recorded.
PermittedAssumptions: Quaternion multiplication and conjugation from Mathlib
FailureCondition: A mismatch with the selected canonical Cayley–Dickson convention or failure of norm composition under the intended field assumptions would require revision.
AuditNotes: This is the independently defined target. The current routed selector data were chosen to reproduce it; Pascal forcing has not yet been proved.
